free_text: Band structure in 180Ta studied with transfer reactions
SD: year in ref. corrected.
SD: Correction in all Subents. Subent 008
deleted (info moved to Subent 007).
Four solid state position sensitive detectors
of 50 mm length each. The energy calibration of the
focal plane was achieved through the 13C(p,d)12C
reaction. The estimated error on the excitation energy
is of the order of 5 keV.
The natural tantalum target, 193 mu-g/cm2 thick, was
obtained by evaporation on a 20 mu-g/cm2 carbon backing
No information about source of uncertainty
Fig.4 from Phys.Rev.,C27(1983)98
SD: ERR-ANALYS and STATUS updated. The
accuracy of data presentation has been revised.
The focal plane detector of 1.40 m length
is a position sensitive telescope of three resistive
wire proportional counters. Elastically scattered 3He
particles were used both for energy calibration and
nonlinearity corrections of the counter. Depending on
the target the overall resolution was 20 to 40 keV
FWHM.
The 179Hf targets were obtained by evaporation of
hafnium oxide enriched to 87% with a main contamination
of 8.55% 180Hf on 20 mu-g/cm2 carbon backing. Targets
of 211 and 148 mu-g/cm2 were used.
No information about source of uncertainty
Fig.6 from Phys.Rev.,C27(1983)98
SD: ERR-ANALYS and STATUS updated. The
accuracy of data presentation has been revised.
Four solid state position sensitive detectors
of 50 mm length each. The energy calibration of the
focal plane was achieved through the 13C(p,d)12C
reaction. The estimated error on the excitation energy
is of the order of 5 keV.
The natural tantalum target, 193 mu-g/cm2 thick, was
obtained by evaporation on a 20 mu-g/cm2 carbon backing
No information on errors is given.
Tbl.IV, p.105 from Phys.Rev.,C27(1983)98
SD: STATUS updated.
The focal plane detector was a 50 cm long
multiwire proportional ionization chamber with spatial
resolution of 0.4 mm. The energy calibration of the
spectrograph was obtained through the Zr(a,t)Nb
reaction.
The 179Hf targets were obtained by evaporation of
hafnium oxide enriched to 87% with a main contamination
of 8.55% 180Hf on 20 mu-g/cm2 carbon backing. Targets
of 211 and 148 mu-g/cm2 were used.
No information on errors is given.
Tbl.VII, p.108 from Phys.Rev.,C27(1983)98
SD: Several values were added from
Subent 006 according to the REACTION code.
